thesis: A ten-mine Americas platform pairing industry-leading silver reserves, record free cash flow and a net-cash balance sheet with major organic options at La Colorada, Timmins, Jacobina, Escobal and Navidad.

source.title: Pan American Silver Investor Presentation - June 2026

whyItMatters.label: Operating scale
whyItMatters.detail: Ten producing silver and gold operations across the Americas, excluding Dolores residual leaching.

whyItMatters.label: Silver reserves
whyItMatters.detail: Company-reported silver mineral reserves as of June 30, 2025, before adding acquired MAG reserves.

whyItMatters.label: Cash generation
whyItMatters.detail: Q1 2026 attributable free cash flow, the highest quarter shown in the presentation.

whyItMatters.label: Financial capacity
whyItMatters.detail: Total available liquidity plus Pan American's attributable cash at Juanicipio at March 31, 2026.

whyItMatters.label: Growth optionality
whyItMatters.detail: La Colorada Skarn, Timmins Camp, Jacobina optimization, Escobal and Navidad create distinct growth paths.

assets.name: La Colorada
assets.description: A 100%-owned vein mine alongside a large skarn project with an initial 37-year mine plan in the revised PEA.

assets.name: Juanicipio
assets.description: A 44%-owned, high-grade primary silver mine with a 3,750 tpd plant and a new underground conveyor system.

assets.name: Jacobina
assets.description: A seven-mine underground complex undergoing plant, tailings and mine optimization across an eight-kilometre trend.

assets.name: El Peñon
assets.production: 104-111 koz gold and 3.65-3.95 Moz silver guidance
assets.description: A 100%-owned underground gold-silver mine with district-scale exploration and spare mill capacity.

assets.name: Timmins
assets.description: Two underground mines and a central plant, with a shaft extension and satellite deposits supporting a district-scale plan.

assets.name: Cerro Moro
assets.production: 2.80-3.00 Moz silver and 80-86 koz gold guidance
assets.description: A mixed open-pit and underground operation where gold by-product credits drive negative silver-segment AISC guidance.

assets.name: Huaron
assets.description: An underground polymetallic mine advancing deeper extensions after commissioning dry-stack tailings infrastructure.

assets.name: Escobal
assets.description: A large, built primary silver mine suspended pending completion of the Guatemalan government's ILO 169 consultation.

assets.name: Navidad
assets.description: A 100%-owned, large undeveloped primary silver deposit whose development depends on a provincial mining-law change.

production.label: 2026 silver guidance
production.detail: Total attributable silver production guidance across the silver and gold segments.

production.label: 2026 gold guidance
production.detail: Total attributable gold production guidance across the diversified portfolio.

production.label: Silver AISC guidance
production.detail: 2026 silver-segment AISC per ounce, net of by-product credits.

production.label: Gold AISC guidance
production.detail: 2026 gold-segment AISC per ounce, net of by-product credits.

scenarios.name: Bull case
scenarios.headline: Record cash flow funds shareholder returns while La Colorada becomes a new tier-one growth engine.
points: Strong metal prices sustain the margin expansion visible in Q1 2026.
The company delivers its targeted 35%-40% return of attributable free cash flow to shareholders.
La Colorada's decline and engineering program de-risk a 37-year expansion plan.
Timmins and Jacobina optimization add durable production beyond current guidance.

scenarios.name: Base case
scenarios.headline: The portfolio delivers guidance and converts strong liquidity into disciplined organic growth.
points: Attributable production lands within 25-27 Moz silver and 700-750 koz gold guidance.
Silver and gold segment AISC remain within the published full-year ranges.
The $515-$550M capital program advances priority projects without stressing the balance sheet.
Dividends, buybacks and project spending remain supported by operating cash generation.

scenarios.name: Bear case
scenarios.headline: Portfolio complexity and capital intensity dilute the benefits of strong silver exposure.
points: Metal prices fall below the assumptions used in 2026 cost guidance.
Higher-cost mines miss operating targets or require additional sustaining capital.
La Colorada, Timmins or Jacobina projects require more capital or longer schedules.
Escobal and Navidad remain unavailable because political and consultation processes do not resolve.

financials.label: Market cap
financials.detail: FactSet market capitalization disclosed by the company as of June 4, 2026.

financials.label: Cash + investments
financials.detail: Cash and investments at March 31, 2026, excluding Pan American's $199M share of Juanicipio cash.

financials.label: Total debt
financials.detail: Company-defined total debt at March 31, 2026, primarily senior notes.

financials.label: Net cash
financials.detail: Calculated as $1.614B cash and investments less $845M total debt; excludes attributable Juanicipio cash.

financials.label: Q1 free cash flow
financials.detail: Attributable free cash flow reported for the first quarter of 2026.

rickRule.label: Survival score
rickRule.detail: $2.56B of total liquidity plus attributable Juanicipio cash and a calculated net-cash position support cycle resilience.

rickRule.label: Asset quality
rickRule.detail: Ten producing mines, 452 Moz of disclosed silver reserves and tier-one-scale options at La Colorada and Escobal.

rickRule.label: Jurisdiction
rickRule.detail: Americas diversification reduces single-country exposure, offset by political constraints at Escobal and Navidad.

rickRule.label: Valuation
rickRule.detail: Calculated research score balancing the disclosed $22.4B market capitalization against record cash flow and growth optionality.

ericSprott.label: Torque score
ericSprott.detail: The shares showed 78% one-year correlation to silver while the portfolio retains major undeveloped silver optionality.

ericSprott.label: Silver leverage
ericSprott.detail: Pan American reports the largest silver reserve position in its peer comparison and guides to 25-27 Moz in 2026.

ericSprott.label: Growth score
ericSprott.detail: La Colorada Skarn, Timmins Camp, Jacobina optimization, Escobal and Navidad offer multiple independent growth paths.

ericSprott.label: Catalyst score
ericSprott.detail: La Colorada decline work, the Timmins PEA, mine optimizations and potential political unlocks create a deep catalyst calendar.

timeline.title: Americas operating platform
timeline.description: Pan American highlights more than three decades operating and building mines across the Americas.

timeline.title: Shahuindo joins the portfolio
timeline.description: The Peru heap-leach gold mine was acquired in 2019 and remains a meaningful contributor to 2026 guidance.

timeline.title: MAG acquisition adds Juanicipio
timeline.description: The acquisition established a 44% interest in one of the world's largest and lowest-cost primary silver mines.

timeline.title: Record financial performance
timeline.description: Attributable revenue reached $1.332B and attributable free cash flow reached $553M.

timeline.title: La Colorada access program
timeline.description: A $265.1M decline program is planned over five years to access and de-risk the skarn ore bodies.

timeline.title: Bell Creek shaft extension target
timeline.description: Commissioning of the Timmins Bell Creek shaft extension is targeted for the first half of 2029.

risks.name: Political
risks.mitigation: The operating portfolio spans multiple countries, but Escobal requires completion of Guatemala's ILO 169 consultation and Navidad depends on provincial law change.

risks.name: Operational
risks.mitigation: Ten operating mines diversify asset risk, while mine-level production and AISC ranges provide transparent execution benchmarks.

risks.name: Commodity price
risks.mitigation: Gold and base-metal by-products diversify revenue, although the investment case remains deliberately leveraged to silver.

risks.name: Balance sheet
risks.mitigation: $1.614B of cash and investments, an undrawn $750M revolver and record free cash flow provide substantial funding capacity.
